gate.title: Admins only
gate.description: Settings — instance configuration, taxonomy management and the role overview — is available to administrators. Ask an admin if you need a change here.

hub.title: Settings
hub.subtitle: Instance configuration, taxonomy management, roles and service accounts — administrators only.

taxonomies.title: Taxonomies
taxonomies.description: Manage the categories that classify assets, applications, consumables and knowledge-base articles — plus asset models.

locations.title: Locations
locations.description: Manage the places your assets physically live — offices, datacenters, racks and storage.

roles.title: Roles
roles.description: See who has which RBAC role across the team. Role changes happen in the Users section.

serviceAccounts.title: Service accounts
serviceAccounts.description: Create and manage non-human API credentials for CI, scripts and integrations — scoped by permission and revocable.

integrations.title: Integrations & workflows
integrations.description: The cross-application manual-task inbox for provisioning workflows. Per-app automation is configured on each application's Workflows tab.

instance.title: Instance
instance.description: Review how this lazyit instance is configured — identity provider, setup state and runtime posture.

instance.title: Instance
instance.subtitle: How this lazyit instance is configured. Read-only — operators set posture via environment.
instance.loadErrorHint: The API may be down or unreachable.

roles.title: Roles
roles.subtitle: Who holds which access level, and what each role can do. Change a role from the Users section.

ADMIN.label: Admin
ADMIN.hint: Full access, including user administration and destructive deletes.

MEMBER.label: Member
MEMBER.hint: Normal inventory, KB and asset operations.

permissions.title: Role permissions
permissions.subtitle: Configure what Members and Viewers can do. Admin always has full access.
permissions.idpNote: These permissions are lazyit-only — they never appear in your identity provider.

segmented.adminTitle: Admin has full access and cannot be edited.

preset.customHint: A one-off set that matches no preset.

fineTune.title: Fine-tune (advanced)
fineTune.subtitle: Exact per-permission control
fineTune.description: Each switch here is a single permission. Changes flip the role to a Custom set and update the capability toggles above.

confirm.title: Review this change before saving
confirm.description: This update has effects worth confirming. Review them, then save.

consequence.removedRead: {who} will no longer be able to {action}.
consequence.accessGrantGrant: {who} will be able to grant and revoke application access for anyone — including to sensitive applications. This is normally an admin-only action.
consequence.userManage: {who} will be able to administer users: create accounts, change roles (including granting ADMIN) and offboard people. This is normally an admin-only action.
consequence.settingsManage: {who} will be able to configure the instance and manage taxonomies — the full admin configuration surface. This is normally an admin-only action.
consequence.deleteDefault: {who} will be able to {action} — a destructive action normally reserved for admins.

serviceAccounts.title: Service accounts
serviceAccounts.subtitle: Non-human API credentials for CI, scripts and integrations — scoped by permission, never a role.

systemManaged.hint: Owned by the workflow engine — every workflow run executes as this account.
systemManaged.locked: System-managed — the workflow engine runs as this account, so it can't be edited, rotated or revoked.

empty.title: No service accounts yet
empty.revokedDescription: Revoked (soft-deleted) accounts appear here and can be restored.
empty.description: Give a CI runner, script or integration scoped, non-human access to the API — create one and hand it a token instead of a person's login.

serviceAccounts.revokeExplanation: Its token stops authenticating immediately. You can restore it from the “Show revoked” view (rotate to mint a fresh token).

form.editDescription: Update the name, permissions and lifecycle of this non-human credential. The token is not changed here — use Rotate for that.
form.newDescription: A non-human credential (a CI runner, a script, an integration). It is authorized by the permissions you grant — never a role. The token is shown once after you create it.
form.descriptionPlaceholder: Optional — what this credential is used for.
form.expiresHint: Optional. After this time the token is rejected. Leave empty for no expiry.
form.activeHint: A soft disable. When off, the token stops authenticating without revoking the account.
form.permissionsHint: The capabilities this account can use. Authorization is by this set — it never inherits a role. Delete and admin-level grants are allowed but flagged.
form.secretDescription: This is the only time the token is shown.

errors.invalidFields: Some fields are invalid. Check the values and try again.

rotate.secretDescription: This is the only time the new token is shown.
rotate.confirmDescription: A new token is minted for {name}. The current token stops working immediately — any system using it must be updated. The new token is shown once.

secretReveal.createdMessage: {name} was created. Here is its token.
secretReveal.rotatedMessage: {name} was rotated. Here is its token.
secretReveal.warning: Copy it now — you won't see it again. We only store a hash of the token, so it can never be shown or recovered later. If you lose it, rotate the account to mint a new one.
secretReveal.acknowledge: I've saved this token in a secure place.

testIt.title: Test it works
testIt.description: Run a quick check from your terminal to confirm the token authenticates and is scoped as expected. Replace the placeholder with the token you copied.
testIt.dialogDescription: Quick checks for “{name}”, based on the permissions it holds. The token is shown as a placeholder — paste in your own.
testIt.noChecks: This account holds no permission that maps to a representative check.
testIt.expect: Expect a {status} on success. Any 401/403 means the token is wrong, inactive, expired, or missing that permission.
testIt.hostNote: Host comes from your API URL. In dev, routes are bare (e.g. /assets). Behind the reverse proxy in production, the public path is /api/* (e.g. /api/assets).
testIt.openApiProd: The OpenAPI explorer is available only in development; it is not exposed publicly in production.

inventory.label: Inventory
inventory.description: Assets, consumables, models, categories and locations.

access.label: Access
access.description: Applications and who can access them.

knowledge.label: Knowledge
knowledge.description: The Knowledge Base — articles and their categories.

manage.label: Manage
manage.description: Users, instance settings, the dashboard and search.

automation.label: Automation
automation.description: The Applications Workflow Engine — workflow definitions, runs, manual tasks and connector credentials.

inventory:view.label: View inventory
inventory:view.description: See assets, consumables, models, categories and locations.

inventory:edit.label: Add & edit inventory
inventory:edit.description: Create and update assets, consumables, models, categories and locations (incl. stock adjustments).

inventory:delete.label: Delete inventory
inventory:delete.description: Archive assets, consumables, models, categories and locations.

accessGrant:view.label: See who has access
accessGrant:view.description: View the access-grant ledger — who can access which applications.

accessGrant:grant.label: Grant & revoke access
accessGrant:grant.description: Open, revoke and edit access grants for any user — including to sensitive applications.

article:edit.label: Write & edit articles
article:edit.description: Create, update and publish Knowledge Base articles.

user:view.label: View the user directory
user:view.description: Browse the list of users (names, emails, roles).

user:manage.label: Administer users
user:manage.description: Create, edit, change roles, offboard and restore users.

dashboardSearch:view.label: View dashboard & search
dashboardSearch:view.description: See the dashboard and use global search.

settings:view.label: View instance settings
settings:view.description: See the settings area (instance configuration, taxonomies).

settings:manage.label: Configure the instance
settings:manage.description: Change instance settings and manage taxonomies — the full admin configuration surface.

workflow:view.label: View workflows & runs
workflow:view.description: See workflow definitions, run history and the manual-task inbox.

workflow:manage.label: Configure workflows & connections
workflow:manage.description: Create, edit, enable and delete workflow definitions and their connections.

workflow:run.label: Run & retry workflows
workflow:run.description: Manually trigger, re-run and retry workflow runs.

workflow:task.label: Complete manual tasks
workflow:task.description: Action and complete manual task steps assigned to you (or your cohort).

workflow:secrets.label: Manage connector credentials
workflow:secrets.description: Configure, enter and rotate per-app connector credentials — kept separate from authoring the workflow logic.

notifications:view.label: View notifications
notifications:view.description: See the in-app notification bell — curated operational nudges (critical-app access, admin elevation, low stock, workflow tasks).

secret:view.label: View the Secret Manager
secret:view.description: See that vaults and secret items exist (names, handles, member lists) — zero-knowledge metadata only; no values are revealed.

secret:manage.label: Manage vaults & memberships
secret:manage.description: Create, edit and delete vaults; manage vault memberships (grant/revoke wrapped DEKs) — the full Secret Manager administration surface.

editor.label: Editor
editor.description: Read and edit everything except deletes and admin actions.

readOnly.label: Read-only
readOnly.description: View access across the app (no edits).

inventoryOperator.label: Inventory operator
inventoryOperator.description: Read and edit Inventory; view-only on Access, Knowledge and Manage.

taxonomies.title: Taxonomies
taxonomies.subtitle: Manage the categories that classify records, plus the asset models assets reference.

categories.emptyDescription: Add your first one to start classifying records — it becomes pickable wherever this kind is referenced.

form.editDescription: Update the details of this category.
form.newDescription: Categories classify records across the app. Name is required; the rest are optional.
form.descriptionPlaceholder: Optional — what belongs in this category.
form.iconPlaceholder: Optional — a heroicon name, e.g. ServerStackIcon

errors.orderInteger: Order must be a whole number.

models.emptyDescription: Add a make and model — say, a MacBook Pro 14 — so every asset you register can reference it instead of retyping the specs.

form.editDescription: Update this make/model and the defaults copied into new assets.
form.newDescription: A generic make/model that assets are instances of, e.g. “Dell Latitude 5520”.

defaultSpecs.label: Default specs
defaultSpecs.description: Fields copied into new assets that use this model. Each asset can still change them.
